text: Upon entering the abdomen, the surgeon observes inflammatory adhesions surrounding a Meckel's diverticulum. The adhesions are carefully dissected using scissors and electrocautery, revealing an area of perforation.

The surgeon identifies and frees the base of the Meckel's diverticulum. An endoscopic stapler is positioned around the base, ensuring the small bowel lumen remains unobstructed. The final attachment of the diverticulum is released.
The surgeon places the specimen, likely the resected Meckel's diverticulum, into an endo bag for removal from the abdominal cavity.
